issue.message: The Compose Compiler cannot infer the stability of a parameter if a List<User> is used in it, even if the item type is stable. You should use Kotlinx Immutable Collections instead: `users: ImmutableList<User>` or create an `@Immutable` wrapper for this class: `@Immutable data class UsersList(val items: List<User>)` See https://slackhq.github.io/compose-lints/rules/#avoid-using-unstable-collections for more information.

The Compose Compiler cannot infer the stability of a parameter if a List<User> is used in it, even if the item type is stable. You should use Kotlinx Immutable Collections instead: `accounts: ImmutableList<User>` or create an `@Immutable` wrapper for this class: `@Immutable data class AccountsList(val items: List<User>)` See https://slackhq.github.io/compose-lints/rules/#avoid-using-unstable-collections for more information.

The Compose Compiler cannot infer the stability of a parameter if a List<User> is used in it, even if the item type is stable. You should use Kotlinx Immutable Collections instead: `users: ImmutableList<User>` or create an `@Immutable` wrapper for this class: `@Immutable data class UsersList(val items: List<User>)` See https://slackhq.github.io/compose-lints/rules/#avoid-using-unstable-collections for more information.
